name: Indivior Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Indivior is a market leader in long-acting injectable medications for opioid use disorder (OUD). It sells SUBLOCADE (buprenorphine extended-release monthly injection) and SUBOXONE Film (buprenorphine and naloxone sublingual film) primarily in the U.S., which accounted for 85% of net revenues in 2025. The company also has operations in Canada, Australia, France, and Germany.

exposures.sentence: Our core products include the following approved treatments: SUBLOCADE (buprenorphine extended-release monthly injection); and SUBOXONE Film (buprenorphine and naloxone sublingual film); both of which are treatments for OUD.

catalysts.event: FDA label expansion for SUBLOCADE including rapid initiation protocol and alternative injection sites
catalysts.sentence: On February 24, 2025, we announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved label changes in the U.S. for SUBLOCADE including a rapid initiation protocol and alternative injection sites.
The last patient, last visit for this study occurred in the fourth quarter of 2025, and we expect to be able to announce results in the second quarter of 2026.
The last patient, last visit for this study was achieved on November 3, 2025, and we expect to be able to announce results in the second quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: In 2024, 1.7% of people aged 12 or older (or 4.8 million people) had a past year OUD.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2024, 1.7% of people aged 12 or older (or 4.8 million people) had a past year OUD.

tamClaims.claim: Only 17.0% (or 818,000 people) received medication for opioid use disorder (MOUD) in the past year.
tamClaims.sentence: Unfortunately, only 17.0% (or 818,000 people) received medication for opioid use disorder (MOUD) in the past year indicating that OUD still carries significant stigma and is often perceived as a moral failing and sign of personal weakness rather than a chronic and relapsing disease that can be managed and responds to treatment.

tamClaims.claim: The 12-month-ending provisional number of reported opioid overdose deaths as of August 2025 was approximately 47,000.
tamClaims.sentence: The 12-month-ending provisional number of reported opioid overdose deaths as of August 2025 was approximately 47,000, which represents approximately 64% of all drug overdose deaths.

tamClaims.claim: 64 million individuals were living with a substance use disorder (SUD) in 2023.
tamClaims.sentence: In the same year, 64 million individuals were living with a substance use disorder (SUD), reflecting a 13% increase over the last 10 years.
